workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Pulling in context from Parnell Aerospace's exec meeting notes -- need to build the analysis for the migration architecture.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 12:18 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Parnell Aerospace

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-02-27 12:49 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 16:44 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Parnell Aerospace

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-L trap (Draft-only default delays response when speed itself is the right move); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-L failure mode (Draft-only default delays response when speed itself is the right move)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-L -- Draft-only default delays response when speed itself is the right move
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
